items.title: Make replication execution-first, producer-neutral, and explicit about catch-up.
acceptance: Every accepted native master opening, partial-close, and full-close execution is copied once per enabled route at the user-configured ratio in Standard and AI, without waiting for a complete bracket or abandoning the copy on a wall-clock timeout.
Turning replication on, enabling a follower, changing a ratio, or changing a master changes configuration only and submits no catch-up, flatten, or re-entry order.
One affirmative UI Sync action is the only owner of catch-up alignment and journals its origin and result.
Manual follower divergence remains intact and never vetoes later master execution deltas.

Replication, Sync, and protection recovery submit only attributable position deltas and cancel or trim only Glitch-owned protection; they never use whole-instrument Flatten to cancel unrelated human orders.
CopyEngine does not infer a follower contract ceiling or apply a non-opt-in compliance gate; separately enabled Settings compliance remains independent and observable.
Both editions pass source contracts and bounded Sim scenarios return every configured account flat and order-free.
evidence: Intent-first rail audit: docs/ledger/audits/2026-07-23-intent-first-rail-audit.md.
Standard source commit standard/20@b1b94f8; npm.cmd test 61/61.
AI source commit ai/22@ae51406; shared npm.cmd test 54/54.
GlitchCopyEngine.cs and GlitchReplicationProtection.cs are byte-identical across Standard and AI; Replication UI drift is limited to named AI executor hooks.
Independent gpt-5.6-sol high review: APPROVE after replication ownership, recovery, and terminal-state corrections.
AI AddOn ai/22@ae51406 deployed once as the complete 91-file GlitchAddOn folder; live hashes match 91/91 and NinjaScript F5 compile exposed no error row or modal.
Deployed UI exposes the explicit per-group Sync control. Deployment submitted no order and preserved the pre-existing Sim102 long 1 and Sim103 long 1 manual divergence.

2026-07-23 native incident evidence: one Sim101 SellShort 1 was processed by retained pre-reload AI windows, producing Sim102 short 8 and Sim103 short 12. The first fleet Flatten directly closed 1/8/12, then two stale copy engines mirrored the master close again and reversed followers to long 4/6, requiring a second operator flatten.

Root cause: cross-assembly GlitchMainWindow instances matched by type name but failed the current-assembly cast; fallback Close was converted to Hide, so old account-event subscriptions remained live after F5 reload.
Standard and AI now invoke the prior assembly's ShutdownForAddOn contract across the reflection boundary; Standard npm.cmd test 62/62, AI shared 55/55, and Hermes 186/186.

AI was redeployed as an exact 91-file source/live set, the source-absent GlitchApexDirectionGuard.cs gate was removed, F5 replaced the old Glitch window handle without an error surface, and read-only telemetry proved all nine visible accounts flat and order-free.
A fresh bounded single-fill replication and one-click Flatten All operator fixture remains pending; Codex did not create a test trade.
items.stop_line: Bounded Sim only. No release, evaluation, PA, live-capital, or external order authority without explicit operator approval.

spec_kit.user_story: As an operator, I want Glitch to mirror the native master actions and UI configuration I chose, while preserving manual follower changes until I explicitly request catch-up.
scenarios: Given an unprotected or not-yet-protected manual master fill, when the native execution arrives, then the configured follower delta is submitted once without a bracket readiness veto.
Given a valid AI master fill whose bracket callback is delayed, when replication is enabled, then the follower copy is not permanently lost to a timeout.
Given existing master/follower divergence, when replication, follower enable, ratio, or master configuration changes, then existing exposure is unchanged.
Given existing divergence, when the operator invokes Sync, then alignment runs once and is journaled.
Given a manual partial master close, when the execution arrives, then each follower closes only the ratio-scaled closable delta.

Given unrelated human exposure or working orders on a follower, when replication closes, syncs, or recovers a failed copied bracket, then only the attributable copied delta and Glitch-owned orders are mutated; ambiguous ownership is journaled and left unchanged.

Given any number of NinjaScript reloads, when one native master execution arrives, then exactly one current AddOn instance processes it and every retired window has released its account-event subscriptions.

Given one explicit Flatten All action, when replication is enabled, then only the current engine is paused and native fleet flatten reaches flat and order-free without a retired engine mirroring the master close.
functional_requirements: Delete pending-copy bracket and TTL gates from the live execution path.
Treat native execution identity and configured route/ratio as the copy authority.
Apply protection when native protection truth is available without making protection discovery a copy veto.
Delete implicit Align call sites and inferred route-cap admission.
Expose one explicit Sync control and observable journal evidence.
Reserve whole-account or whole-instrument Flatten for the explicit fleet Flatten All command; use exact named deltas in replication-owned paths.
Keep Standard and AI shared behavior parity-proven.
Permanently close prior-assembly Glitch windows during NinjaScript replacement; ordinary user close may retain the current AI runtime, but replacement must unsubscribe the retired instance.
success_criteria: Zero implicit alignment orders across four configuration paths.
Exactly one follower mutation per unique master execution and route.
Repeated NinjaScript reloads leave exactly one current Glitch window and one account-event subscriber set.
One Flatten All click cannot be counter-traded by a retired replication engine.
No PendingMasterCopyTtl, FollowerFinalAdmissionUnavailable, or FollowerFinalMaxContracts gate remains in CopyEngine.
All bounded fixtures finish flat and order-free.
assumptions: Manual follower divergence is intentional until explicit Sync.
Ratio changes apply to future execution deltas until explicit Sync.
Factual native order rejection is evidence, not permission to invent a different trade.
tasks: Add failing source-contract tests for bracketless copy, no timeout, no inferred route cap, and config-only UI changes.
Implement the smallest shared CopyEngine change in Standard.
Add the explicit Sync UI action and localization.
Apply the shared fix to AI and prove intended parity.
Run repeated-reload single-execution and one-click fleet-flatten regression fixtures.
Run bounded manual and AI Sim callback fixtures.

items.title: Guarantee one execution owner per intent UUID and body.
acceptance: One hundred or more simultaneous identical intent POSTs cause exactly one executor invocation; all other callers receive in-progress or immutable replay state.
The same UUID with different content returns conflict and causes zero additional mutation.
A restart from received, approved, or execution-started state reconciles native truth without duplicate submission.
Execution ownership and state transitions are atomic and durable on the local single-machine authority.
evidence: AI source commit ai/22@ae51406.
AI shared suite 54/54 and Hermes suite 186/186.
Atomic UUID plus body ownership, immutable terminal replay, and same-body concurrency/restart source fixtures pass.
Independent gpt-5.6-sol high review: APPROVE after terminal callback/server race was consolidated through one locked durable transition.
Native restart fault-injection remains pending coupled AI deployment authority.
items.stop_line: Bounded local and Sim verification only. No external or live intent authority.

spec_kit.user_story: As an operator, I need retries and concurrent delivery of one intent to create at most one native mutation.
scenarios: Given simultaneous identical UUID/body requests, when ownership is claimed, then one request executes and all others observe in-progress or replay.
Given the same UUID with different content, when claimed, then conflict is terminal and no order is created.
Given a crash at each nonterminal phase, when the service restarts, then native state is reconciled without resubmission.
functional_requirements: Use one atomic ownership transition under the state-store lock.
Bind ownership to the durable body hash.
Make terminal replay immutable.
Remove resume-approved and unconditional execution-started races.
success_criteria: Exactly one executor call under a barrier-synchronized 100-request fixture.
Zero duplicate journal terminal records.
Restart fixtures never create a second native signal.
assumptions: The localhost server and local state directory are the single-machine intent authority.
NinjaTrader native orders and positions resolve crash ambiguity.
tasks: Freeze the intent transition table.
Add a concurrent duplicate fixture.
Implement the atomic ownership API and remove the old branching.
Add received, approved, and execution-started fault-injection tests.
Run bounded restart reconciliation.

items.title: Keep submitted intents nonterminal until native outcome is proven.
acceptance: A synchronous Submit return records pending execution, not terminal executed.
An entry becomes executed exactly once only after exact native master exposure and working native protection are proven.
A pending duplicate POST reconciles the same UUID and never starts a second execution.
The direct worker preserves the same outbox while native outcome is pending and does not mark the model attempt completed.
Reject, terminal partial fill, late fill, protection failure, and crash-after-submit fixtures produce truthful failed, recovered, or executed evidence and final flat/order-free state.
evidence: AI source commit ai/22@ae51406.
AI shared suite 54/54 and Hermes suite 186/186.
ENTRY and EXIT durable ownership/recovery fixtures cover pending submit, delayed callbacks, partial or rejected outcomes, crash-before-plan, crash-after-plan, and human interleave.
Independent gpt-5.6-sol high review: APPROVE; safely unwound protection failure remains truthful failed state rather than executed.
Native bounded Sim callback and restart lifecycle proof remains pending coupled AI deployment authority.
items.stop_line: No unattended, evaluation, PA, or live-readiness claim until callback and restart fixtures pass.

spec_kit.user_story: As an operator, I want receipts and retries to report native protected truth rather than treating order submission as execution.
scenarios: Given Submit returns before callbacks, when the server responds, then state remains pending.
Given the entry fills and exact native brackets work, when callbacks reconcile, then executed commits once.
Given reject, terminal partial fill, late fill, or protection failure, when recovery completes, then no stale executed result remains.
Given a crash after submit, when the same UUID is retried, then native state is reconciled without another submit.
functional_requirements: Reuse execution_started as durable pending state.
Give one callback/reconciliation path ownership of terminal execution.
Represent pending in the HTTP receipt and direct-worker classification.
Require native exact exposure plus working protection for terminal entry success.
success_criteria: No terminal executed record exists without group_entry_open_protected evidence.
Pending delivery reuses the same intent UUID and outbox.
Every failure fixture ends flat and order-free.
assumptions: Polling or same-UUID reconciliation must not block the NinjaTrader UI thread.
tasks: Define pending receipt semantics.
Connect native callback completion to durable state.
Change direct-worker pending classification.
Add delayed fill, rejection, partial, late-fill, and crash fixtures.
Run bounded Sim lifecycle proof.

items.title: Restore human and Hermes authority over deterministic policy.
acceptance: A complete due packet invokes Luna according to cadence even when operational facts make a new entry currently ineligible.
Operational, health, and compliance facts remain visible in the packet and may produce a factual executor rejection, but they do not suppress cognition.
Inferred prop-firm contract ceilings, Apex buffers, sessions, or time windows do not override a valid AI or human intent unless the user enabled the corresponding Settings compliance action.

AI recovery and EXIT mutate only correlation-attributable AI exposure and orders; a same-instrument human add, partial close, full close, opposite trade, or unrelated working order supersedes ambiguous AI mutation.
NOTHING, rejection, and non-executed decisions remain attributable learning evidence.
evidence: AI source commit ai/22@ae51406.
AI shared suite 54/54 and Hermes suite 186/186.
Pre-model eligibility suppression and inferred quantity, Apex, session, time-window, and stop-widening gates were removed from non-opt-in authority paths.
AI recovery and EXIT use UUID/correlation-attributable deltas and preserve ambiguous human changes.
Public Hermes v0.0.2.9 commit glitch-hermes-profile@744eb30 and tag v0.0.2.9 are pushed; installed profile passes 28/28 distribution-owned hash parity with plugin and supervised gateway present.
AI profile source ai/22@4c13209 now reproduces the public v0.0.2.9 tree exactly and removes the stale embedded deterministic capacity/session language that the drift check exposed.
AI and both preserved Hermes jobs remained paused through profile update and AddOn deployment.

Fresh epoch eb99193e-8a85-4908-ab37-75f3e8937a35 reset without backup after all nine accounts were proven flat/order-free; post-reset memory, request dumps, intents, trade ledger, risk locks, and learning streams are empty; AddOn reload established fresh 250k account-peak baselines.
Native Hermes and human-interleave Sim proof remains pending because the existing follower positions were preserved rather than commandeered for a test.
items.stop_line: No cognition-policy promotion or performance claim from synthetic evidence.

spec_kit.user_story: As the human operator, I want Hermes to own trading decisions while Glitch enforces only factual executability and compliance actions I explicitly enabled.
scenarios: Given a due flat packet and a closed window, risk lock, unavailable native state, or working order, when cadence is due, then Luna still produces an attributable decision.
Given Hermes emits a factually invalid action, when posted, then the executor rejects it with zero mutation.
Given an inferred prop-firm limit but no matching Settings opt-in, when a valid intent is submitted, then the inference is observational and does not veto it.

Given human same-instrument activity after an AI entry, when AI recovery or EXIT runs, then only provably AI-attributable exposure and orders are mutated; ambiguity produces a visible superseded outcome and no mutation.
functional_requirements: Delete deterministic flat-book eligibility suppression from model cadence.
Separate factual invalidity from inferred or optional compliance policy.
Bind every automated compliance veto or action to a visible default-off Settings control.
Replace whole-instrument AI recovery and EXIT flattening with correlation-owned cancellation and exact attributable close deltas.
Preserve decision and rejection learning evidence.
success_criteria: All former pre-model eligibility cases invoke Luna when cadence is due.
Invalid actions still create zero native order.
No non-opt-in prop-firm inference blocks replication or AI master execution.
assumptions: Model spend does not outrank the explicit human > AI > determinism hierarchy.
Broker-native rejection remains authoritative factual evidence.
tasks: Freeze the cognition versus execution truth table.
Delete the flat-book eligibility veto and update tests.
Inventory firewall and executor gates as factual, observational, or user-opt-in.
Remove or bind non-factual gates to visible Settings consent.
Prove NOTHING and rejection learning continuity.

items.title: Require visible persisted opt-in before automated compliance flatten.
acceptance: AI enable and account allowlisting do not imply consent to forced daily-close orders.
The daily-close compliance setting is visible, specific, persisted, audited, and false by default.
With the setting off, daily close causes zero order mutation; with it on, only the visibly scoped configured accounts are acted on once.
Restart preserves consent without expanding scope, and unresolved accounts produce observable warnings.
evidence: AI source commit ai/22@ae51406.
Default-off daily-close setting is visible, persisted, independently scoped, and localized in all six authored locales.
AI shared suite 54/54 and Hermes suite 186/186 include off, on, persistence, scope, and unresolved-account source contracts.
Native deployed Settings UI exposes the account-scoped daily-close control unchecked; no compliance order was submitted.
Native enabled bounded Sim proof remains pending because enabling a broad flatten control would mutate the preserved follower positions.
items.stop_line: No automated compliance flatten outside bounded Sim until the explicit setting and scope are verified.

spec_kit.user_story: As an operator, I want compliance automation to act only after I separately opt in through Settings.
scenarios: Given default settings or AI-only enablement, when daily close arrives, then no account is flattened.
Given explicit daily-close opt-in and exposed selected accounts, when the boundary arrives, then scoped flatten runs once.
Given restart or a disconnected account, when enforcement runs, then consent scope is preserved and unresolved state is visible.
functional_requirements: Add a specific default-false persisted setting.
Expose it in Settings with clear action text.
Guard the daily-close path before account expansion.
Journal enable, disable, action, and unresolved outcomes.
success_criteria: Zero forced order with the setting off.
No non-selected account mutation with the setting on.
Enabled bounded Sim fixture ends flat and order-free.
assumptions: AI enable and allowlisting are not compliance consent.
Existing entry-window facts may remain observational unless separately enabled as enforcement.
tasks: Add persistence and migration for the specific setting.
Add localized Settings UI.
Guard and journal daily-close enforcement.
Add off, on, restart, disconnected, and scope tests.
Run bounded Sim proof.

items.title: Make the AI minute publisher scale with minutes instead of timer ticks.
acceptance: After the current minute frame and packet exist, later one-second ticks perform zero portfolio captures, snapshot reads, frame reads, or packet hashes for that minute.
An incomplete current minute remains retriable until one coherent immutable frame and packet exist.
Under the 25-account bounded fixture, publisher dispatcher time is p95 below 10 ms, p99 below 25 ms, and maximum below 50 ms; native collection lock p99 is below 5 ms.
Thirty-minute allocation and IO counts scale with minutes rather than timer ticks without changing packet contents or cadence.
evidence: AI source commit ai/22@ae51406.
Publisher preflight, cache-hit early exit, lease release, background IO/hash/serialization, and dispatcher/native-lock timing instrumentation pass source fixtures.
AI shared suite 54/54 and Hermes suite 186/186.
The observed NinjaTrader environment exposes nine accounts; the required 25-account, 30-minute native measurement is not yet proven and this ticket remains in progress.
items.stop_line: Performance work must not alter native order behavior, evidence contents, or decision cadence.

spec_kit.user_story: As a NinjaTrader operator, I need AI evidence publication to stay out of the UI and order-callback hot path.
scenarios: Given the minute is already published, when another timer tick fires, then publication returns before capture or file IO.
Given the current minute is incomplete, when later coherent data appears, then publication retries and creates exactly one frame and packet.
Given rollover or restart, when publication resumes, then immutable packet identity and contents remain correct.
functional_requirements: Make portfolio capture lazy.
Check current minute and packet existence before native locks or frame IO.
Keep retry state thread-safe and atomic-write aware.
Add timing and operation counters for bounded verification.
success_criteria: Zero redundant post-publish work.
At most one successful frame and packet build per minute.
Publisher ready within two seconds of complete coherent input.
Measured dispatcher and lock targets pass.
assumptions: Atomic final file existence proves a completed immutable artifact.
Incomplete or failed writes remain eligible for retry.
tasks: Instrument a before baseline.
Add lazy capture and early-exit ownership.
Add rollover, incomplete, and write-failure tests.
Run 10, 25, and 50-account bounded load fixtures.
Record before and after evidence.

items.title: Reconcile internal, public, and ABKB Glitch authority documentation.
acceptance: Internal rail, public AddOn documentation, public Hermes profile facts, and ABKB all state the same human > AI > determinism hierarchy and explicit catch-up/compliance semantics.
The AI rail states Hermes profile v0.0.2.9 and the 30-minute learning cadence proven by distribution source.
A drift check detects synthetic version, cadence, or shared replication-contract mismatch.
No documentation claims release, profitability, unattended operation, or live readiness without named evidence and approval.
evidence: Durable authority audit: docs/ledger/audits/2026-07-23-intent-first-rail-audit.md.
Durable internal/public contracts and all six authored public locales pass npm.cmd test 46/46.
ABKB authority update commit projects/abkb@9801289.
Standard source commit standard/20@b1b94f8 and AI source commit ai/22@ae51406.
AI profile source reconciliation commit ai/22@4c13209; public-profile rebuild is byte-clean against glitch-hermes-profile@744eb30.
Public Hermes v0.0.2.9 commit glitch-hermes-profile@744eb30 and tag v0.0.2.9 are pushed; installed profile passes 28/28 distribution-owned hash parity.
Hermes plugin and supervised gateway are present; both persisted jobs and Glitch AI remain paused.
Independent gpt-5.6-sol high review: APPROVE.
items.stop_line: Documentation and checks only; no release or publication without explicit operator approval.

spec_kit.user_story: As an operator and maintainer, I need one current explanation of what Glitch may do and which component owns each decision.
scenarios: Given public profile v0.0.2.9, when docs are checked, then version and 30-minute cadence agree everywhere.
Given a replication or compliance behavior change, when docs are reconciled, then internal, public, and ABKB authority text agrees.
Given a synthetic mismatch, when the drift check runs, then it fails before merge.
functional_requirements: Update stale release and cadence facts.
Document explicit Sync and default-off compliance.
Document human > AI > deterministic hierarchy without weakening native factual truth.
Automate drift detection for durable facts.
success_criteria: No known authority or release-fact drift remains.
Synthetic drift is detected by an automated check.
Every operational claim links to source or bounded evidence.
assumptions: Public profile commit 38a9292 is current profile source truth.
This task changes source and documentation, not release artifacts.
tasks: Update durable north star and AI rail.
Update Standard and AI public documentation in all authored languages.
Update ABKB profile and ontology.
Add a durable drift check.
Record exact branch and test evidence.

items.title: Reduce Standard and AI lane drift only along touched ownership seams.
acceptance: All shared AddOn paths are classified as identical or intentional edition delta.
Touched shared fixes are applied Standard-first and parity-proven in AI.
Any extraction deletes duplicate branching or parsing and does not become a speculative shared-package rewrite.
No unexplained drift remains in a touched ownership seam.
evidence: Standard source commit standard/20@b1b94f8; npm.cmd test 61/61.
AI source commit ai/22@ae51406; shared npm.cmd test 54/54 and Hermes npm.cmd test 186/186.
Shared GlitchCopyEngine.cs and GlitchReplicationProtection.cs are byte-identical across editions; the Replication UI difference is the named AI executor hook only.
CopyEngine net growth versus lane HEAD is 701 lines after consolidating route lookup, signal parsing, submission results, and observational recovery construction.
Independent gpt-5.6-sol high review: APPROVE and final simplification re-review: Lean already. Ship.
AI AddOn ai/22@ae51406 deployed once as the complete 91-file GlitchAddOn folder; live hashes match 91/91 and NinjaScript F5 compile exposed no error row or modal.
Native bounded Sim parity remains pending because the pre-existing Sim102 and Sim103 long positions were preserved.
items.stop_line: No behavior-neutral mega-refactor before the bounded P0 safety fixtures pass.

spec_kit.user_story: As a maintainer, I want shared fixes to remain identical across editions without risking a speculative rewrite.
scenarios: Given a touched shared file, when parity is checked, then any difference is intentional and named.
Given a proposed extraction, when reviewed, then it removes existing duplicate ownership and preserves behavior.
functional_requirements: Maintain a shared-path parity report.
Use Standard as the shared-source donor.
Extract only proven ownership seams.
Reject unexplained touched-file drift.
success_criteria: Every touched shared path is identical or documented.
No safety behavior changes solely to reduce file size.
Both edition suites remain green.
assumptions: Standard and AI remain separate release lanes.
A shared binary/package is not required for the current safety repair.
tasks: Create a parity manifest/check.
Classify current shared deltas.
Apply fixes Standard-first.
Extract only when a touched seam becomes simpler.
Run independent review in both lanes.

items.title: Keep Standard v0.0.2.0 no-regression evidence tied to its exact inspected artifact.
acceptance: Source, F5 compile evidence, exported artifact hash, release catalog entry, and customer-facing channel agree for Standard v0.0.2.0.
Shared fixes are independently verified in Standard and AI source before either release changes.
No release or public claim is made without operator approval and named evidence.
items.stop_line: No release, production publication, live-capital authority, broker credential change, or customer claim without explicit operator approval and exact artifact evidence.

items.title: Runtime-prove intent v3 per-leg TP/SL amendments and independent additions.
acceptance: In bounded Sim, changing one selected leg leaves sibling legs unchanged.
Follower-native protection mirrors the accepted master change and every account returns flat and order-free.
Rejected or stale input causes zero order mutation and a deterministic evidence record.
items.stop_line: Bounded Sim only. No evaluation, PA, live capital, unattended promotion, release, or external order authority without explicit operator approval.

items.title: Runtime-prove safe widening and deterministic rejection outside authoritative Apex capacity.
acceptance: A widening request inside authoritative current capacity succeeds with complete protection evidence.
A request outside capacity mutates no order and records the exact rejection reason.
Master and follower state reconcile flat and order-free after the bounded fixture.
items.stop_line: Bounded Sim only. No evaluation, PA, live capital, unattended promotion, release, or external order authority without explicit operator approval.

items.title: Prove hidden-window continuity, next-packet retry, stale-lock recovery, and crash reconciliation.
acceptance: Hidden or minimized UI does not stop the bounded runtime pipeline.
Recognized failure retries only on the next eligible packet and does not duplicate intent execution.
Restart recovery preserves truthful health, idempotent intent state, native protection, and final flat/order-free reconciliation.
items.stop_line: Bounded Sim only. No evaluation, PA, live capital, unattended promotion, release, or external order authority without explicit operator approval.

items.title: Prove learning continuity from NOTHING, rejection, and authoritative outcome episodes.
acceptance: Atomic deduplicated decision episodes reach the intended hourly, 300-minute, and daily learning loops exactly once.
Infrastructure faults and incomplete account outcomes never become strategy memory.
A later bounded cycle can retrieve evidence-linked learning without treating stale decisions as current authority.
items.stop_line: No policy promotion, strategy claim, unattended trading, evaluation, PA, or live use from synthetic or incomplete learning evidence.

items.title: Freeze an attributable paper sample before altering cognition or making performance claims.
acceptance: The sample joins NinjaTrader and Glitch executions to exact packet, decision, protection, follower, and outcome evidence.
Inclusion, exclusion, time window, regime, and missing-data rules are frozen before evaluation.
Results report uncertainty and limitations and do not imply profitability or readiness beyond the observed paper evidence.
items.stop_line: No cognition change, fixed strategy extraction, profitability claim, evaluation, PA, or live promotion from an unfrozen or unattributable sample.

items.title: Establish authoritative holiday and special-close truth.
acceptance: A named authoritative source covers holidays, early closes, timezone/DST behavior, and source failure.
Deterministic tests prove trading is refused when the source is stale, incomplete, or contradictory.
The operator approves any authority-source or policy change before promotion.
items.stop_line: No unattended, evaluation, PA, or live-readiness claim while holiday or special-close truth is incomplete.
items.blocked_reason: No complete authoritative holiday and special-close source is currently bound to the runtime contract.

items.title: Prove dependency and restart recovery across the maintained AI runtime.
acceptance: Provider, platform, network, process, and machine-restart failures each produce deterministic safe state and evidence.
Recovery never duplicates an intent, loses native protection, or reports healthy before dependencies and state agree.
Final fixtures reconcile every configured account flat and order-free.
items.stop_line: No unattended, evaluation, PA, or live-readiness claim before named dependency and restart recovery fixtures pass.

items.title: Design and prove a complete pending limit-entry lifecycle before allowing AI limit entries.
acceptance: The contract covers place, cancel, replace, TIF or expiry, partial-fill protection, replication, durable identity, and restart recovery.
Every failure path preserves native protection and idempotent final reconciliation.
Independent review and bounded Sim evidence precede any schema or runtime promotion.
items.stop_line: AI entries remain market-only until the full pending-entry lifecycle is independently verified and explicitly approved.

extensions.selection_rule: Select the highest-priority dependency-clear ready item allowed by its branch and stop line. No eligible item means a clean stop without a write.

model_policy.rule: Use Sol for bounded architecture and adversarial review, Terra for implementation and verification, and Luna only for Hermes runtime cognition; do not spend Sol extra-high on the whole rail.

resolved_ambiguities: AI enable or account allowlisting is not consent to forced compliance orders; each compliance action requires a visible default-off Settings opt-in.
Model-cost suppression does not outrank human > AI > determinism; due cognition is not skipped by hidden deterministic eligibility gates.
